This technique effectively manipulates the mesosalpinx, uterine ovarian ligament, and vessels up to the round ligament, avoiding the ureter posteriorly.

The surgeon uses a hook to create a small window in the lateral one-third of the round ligament, applying counter traction. The surgeon performs posterior dissection of the broad ligament during a hysterectomy, with the uterus lifted for efficiency. The surgeon performs an early colpotomy incision to release the endopelvic fascia surrounding the uterine vessels, making them appear more plump and easier to seal.
